clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Weight loss recommendation for BP management in adults with overweight or obesity
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial evaluation of elevated BP or hypertension in adults with BMI ≥25 (overweight) or BMI ≥30 (obesity)
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend minimum 5% body weight reduction to prevent or treat elevated BP/hypertension

navigationPath.purpose: Calculate BMI and determine overweight/obesity status
Assess baseline BP (elevated or hypertensive range)
Display COR 1, LOE A recommendation for weight loss
Present weight loss strategies (diet, exercise, behavioral)
Track weight loss progress toward 5% goal
Re-evaluate BP after weight loss achievement

currentWeight.validation: Valid weight in kg or lbs

height.validation: Valid height in cm or inches

validationRules: BMI ≥25 for overweight or ≥30 for obesity required
BP must be elevated (SBP 120-129 and DBP <80) or hypertensive (≥130/≥80)

rules: If BMI ≥25 (overweight) or ≥30 (obesity) AND BP elevated/hypertensive → apply recommendation
Recommend lifestyle interventions (dietary modification, increased physical activity, behavioral counseling)
Monitor BP response to weight loss at 3-6 month intervals
If 5% weight loss achieved and BP still elevated → consider additional interventions

recommendation.description: Weight loss recommended with goal of at least 5% reduction

followUpInterval.description: 3-6 months for weight and BP reassessment

appDisplay.screenType: Standard recommendation display with COR/LOE badge
appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing to weight management plan

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Implement weight loss program with dietary and physical activity counseling
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Reassess weight and BP at 3 months, then every 3-6 months until goal achieved
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor weight monthly, BP at each clinical visit
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: If no weight loss after 6 months, escalate to nutritionist or obesity medicine specialist
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log baseline weight, goal weight, BP, and follow-up schedule to health record
